items.title: Critical RCE in widely deployed VPN appliance — patch advisory issued
items.description: A pre-auth remote code execution vulnerability has been disclosed in a popular enterprise VPN appliance. Vendor has issued an emergency patch and is urging all customers to update immediately. Indicators of compromise are circulating; assume breach if exposure precedes the patch window.

items.title: CVE-2026-1124: Linux kernel use-after-free in netfilter
items.description: NVD has published CVE-2026-1124, a use-after-free condition in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem allowing local privilege escalation on affected versions. CVSS 7.8.

items.title: Ransomware crew rebrands again, drops new affiliate program
items.description: Researchers tracked the latest rebrand of a long-running ransomware crew. New affiliate terms favor large-payout enterprise targets and offer pre-built initial-access kits.

items.title: Phishing-as-a-service kit observed targeting Okta and Google Workspace
items.description: A new PhaaS kit specifically designed to harvest Okta and Google Workspace credentials with passkey-bypass workflows is now widely distributed on darkweb forums.

items.title: CISA adds five new vulnerabilities to Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
items.description: Federal civilian agencies have 21 days to remediate. The new entries include three N-day flaws in widely deployed perimeter products.

items.title: Why "assume breach" is the only defensible posture in 2026
items.description: An opinion piece arguing that perimeter-first thinking is dead. The author makes the case for telemetry, segmentation, and recoverability as the new pillars.

items.title: Inside an APT campaign: dwell time, lateral movement, and the slow exfil
items.description: A vendor research team published a deep technical analysis of a multi-month intrusion at a critical-infrastructure target. Includes ATT&CK mapping and high-confidence IOCs.

items.title: HackerOne disclosure: $48,000 bounty for chained zero-day in payment SDK
items.description: A researcher chained three vulnerabilities in a popular payment SDK to achieve full account takeover with no user interaction. Now patched.

items.title: Browser zero-day exploited in the wild, emergency update issued
items.description: A type-confusion vulnerability in the V8 engine is being actively exploited via watering-hole attacks against journalists and activists. Update immediately.

items.title: Shadow IT discovery: how a small change to DNS logs exposed 2,400 unsanctioned services
items.description: A defender writes up how a 30-line script over DNS logs surfaced thousands of forgotten apps, abandoned subdomains, and unmonitored data flows.

items.title: PortSwigger Research: bypassing modern WAFs with parser confusion
items.description: New research detailing a class of parser-confusion attacks that reliably defeat the cloud WAFs in the top 5 vendors. PoCs and detections included.

items.title: Sophos: ransomware dwell time hits 8 days, down from 15 in 2024
items.description: Annual report shows operators are moving faster from initial access to encryption. Key takeaway: hours, not days, for detection windows.

items.title: Major MFA provider details root cause of last week's outage
items.description: A detailed post-mortem covers the misconfigured certificate rotation that locked tens of millions of users out for 2.5 hours. Lessons for cert-pinning architectures.

items.title: New research: training data poisoning in commercial LLMs is more practical than thought
items.description: Academic + industry team demonstrates a low-cost method to poison commercial LLM behaviors with under 0.1% of training data. Detection remains an open problem.
